headline: Ford recalls about 1.4 million F-150 pickups over gearshift issue
summary: STORY: :: FordFord on Friday said it is recalling about 1.4 million of its popular F-150 pickup trucks in the U.S. following an investigation by federal regulators into reports of unexpected downshifts in the vehicles.The U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, or NHTSA, said Ford was aware of two "potentially" related injuries and one accident.It added that dealers would update the trucks' powertrain control module software as a remedy.Earlier this year, NHTSA had expanded a safety-related investigation into the issue.:: FordA preliminary evaluation was first opened in March of 2025, after receiving complaints related to F-150 pickups from 2015 to 2017 equipped with the '6R80' transmission.Ford had previously said that the issue may have been caused by electrical connections wearing down over time due to heat and vibration, leading to signal loss from the transmission range sensor.NHTSA said that incorrect signals likely lead to an unintended downshift.Ford’s F-Series trucks have long been the bestselling automobile in America.Shares of Ford were up more than 4% in Friday afternoon trading.

raw.headline: Ford recalls about 1.4 million F-150 pickups over gearshift issue
raw.summary: STORY: :: FordFord on Friday said it is recalling about 1.4 million of its popular F-150 pickup trucks in the U.S. following an investigation by federal regulators into reports of unexpected downshifts in the vehicles.The U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, or NHTSA, said Ford was aware of two "potentially" related injuries and one accident.It added that dealers would update the trucks' powertrain control module software as a remedy.Earlier this year, NHTSA had expanded a safety-related investigation into the issue.:: FordA preliminary evaluation was first opened in March of 2025, after receiving complaints related to F-150 pickups from 2015 to 2017 equipped with the '6R80' transmission.Ford had previously said that the issue may have been caused by electrical connections wearing down over time due to heat and vibration, leading to signal loss from the transmission range sensor.NHTSA said that incorrect signals likely lead to an unintended downshift.Ford’s F-Series trucks have long been the bestselling automobile in America.Shares of Ford were up more than 4% in Friday afternoon trading.

headline: Tesla Stock Set to Snap 8-Week Losing Streak—Just in Time For Earnings
summary: Tesla  stock was advancing early Friday and is on course to snap an extended losing streak.  Shares in the electric-vehicle maker were up 1.4% to $394.53 in premarket trading.  Alongside broader equity markets, Tesla stock appears to have been lifted by news earlier in the week that the U.S. and Iran are looking to extend their two-week cease-fire beyond April 22.

headline: Is Ford (F) Fairly Priced After Its 1 Year 35.8% Share Price Gain
summary: This article examines whether Ford Motor's current share price aligns with its underlying value by walking through what the numbers may indicate about the stock. Ford's shares most recently closed at US$12.44, with returns of 1.6% over 7 days, 4.2% over 30 days, a 6.7% decline year to date, and 35.8% over 1 year, as well as 26.6% over 3 years and 38.8% over 5 years. Recent coverage has focused on how Ford fits into the broader auto sector and how investors are weighing its position against...
